corpora.description: 761 production-merged Prisma-style Postgres migrations from three established open-source repositories. First calibration corpus used for verify's migration vertical. Canonical corpus for DM-18.

sources.name: cal.com
sources.notes: Largest slice of the corpus. Prisma-generated Postgres migrations with long history going back to 2021. NOTE (2026-04-15): cal.com announced closure of their core open-source codebase. This corpus is a frozen artifact from the open-source era. The snapshot SHA should be pinned to the last public commit before closure. formbricks and supabase remain open and reproducible.

sources.name: supabase
sources.notes: Hand-written Supabase migration examples from the supabase repo. Contains native Postgres migrations across multiple example projects.

suitability.suitability_reason: Production-merged filters out grounding-shape failures (DM-01..05) because migrations that reference non-existent tables or columns would have failed CI or code review and never been merged. Prisma's defensive migration generator pre-empts safety-shape failures (DM-15, DM-16, DM-17, DM-19) by emitting constraint drops before column or table drops, by computing type changes through intermediate columns rather than direct narrowings, and by managing indexes through named constraint definitions. DM-18 is the exception because it fires on a runtime data failure (NOT NULL without DEFAULT against a non-empty table) that no generator can pre-empt and no code review can catch without knowing row counts.

reproducibility.notes: The corpus is reproduced by running backtest.ts to clone the three repos, then replay-engine.ts to run the grounding and safety gates against each migration in sequence. cal.com closed their core codebase in April 2026; the corpus is a frozen artifact from the open-source era. formbricks and supabase remain publicly clonable. The snapshot SHA for cal.com should be pinned to the last public commit before closure.

limitations: Prisma-heavy: 730 of 761 files are Prisma-generated. Results on hand-written SQL, Django, Rails, or Alembic migrations are not measured.
Production-merged: the corpus contains only migrations that passed CI and code review, which structurally filters out many failure modes that exist in pre-merge or draft states.
Single framework family: all three repos use Prisma or hand-written Supabase migrations. The corpus does not exercise migration generators from other ecosystems.
cal.com closed their core codebase in April 2026. The corpus is a frozen artifact. Snapshot SHA for the last public commit needs to be pinned. formbricks and supabase remain open and reproducible.

corpora.description: 345 Django migrations from the Read the Docs project (readthedocs.org), spanning 16 Django apps. First Django corpus used in verify's calibration discipline. Sourced because the three-corpus Django comparison (Saleor, Misago, Read the Docs) identified Read the Docs as the provenance-cleanest source with the fewest false-positive amplifiers (zero SeparateDatabaseAndState state-only ops, zero manual RunSQL DROP CONSTRAINT workarounds).

sources.name: readthedocs
sources.notes: Sparse-checkout of the readthedocs/ subtree. 345 migration files across 16 Django apps (acl, allauth, analytics, api, audit, aws, builds, config, core, doc_builder, domains, embed, filetreediff, forms, gold, integrations, invitations, and more). 100% of migrations declare safe = Safe.<method>() via django_safemigrate, which is independent evidence for the DM-28 candidate shape.

suitability.suitability_reason: DM-15 measured under pre-registered bar on 2026-04-14 and produced zero canonical DM-15 findings (incoming-FK dependents sub-case). 4 findings fired on the outgoing-FK warning sub-case but all 4 were classified FP due to a parser modeling gap: Django emits an implicit DROP CONSTRAINT before DROP COLUMN at SQL compile time for FK-bearing columns, which the AST-level parser does not model. See attempt dm15-django-readthedocs-v1-2026-04-14 in attempts.jsonl. Other shapes not yet measured against this corpus.

reproducibility.notes: Reproduced by running django-parser.py against the readthedocs/ subtree to emit normalized JSONL, then django-runner.ts to topologically order by Migration.dependencies, convert Django ops to typed MigrationOps, build schema state progressively via applyOp, and feed each file through runSafetyGate. Clone SHA is not pinned; re-running will pull the latest main branch and may drift from the April 2026 snapshot.

limitations: Single project: the corpus is one Django project, not a multi-repo aggregate. Results generalize to Read the Docs specifically, not to the broader Django ecosystem.

Parser modeling gap: Django's implicit DROP CONSTRAINT before DROP COLUMN on FK-bearing columns is not modeled by the AST-level parser, which caused 4 outgoing-FK-sub-case DM-15 findings to fire as false positives. The canonical incoming-FK sub-case is unaffected by this gap because it depends on fkIn at schema state, not on whether an outgoing FK has been dropped.

AlterField type extraction deferred: DM-17 (narrowing type change) cannot be measured against this corpus until the parser extracts new field types from AlterField operations. 273 AlterField operations are present but currently skipped.
Clone SHA not pinned: re-running the clone reproduces against current main of readthedocs/readthedocs.org and will drift over time.

Meta.db_table overrides not handled: the parser assumes Django's default {app_label}_{model_name_lower} table naming convention. Models that override with class Meta: db_table would map to incorrect table names. Spot checks did not surface any but the limitation stands.

candidates.description: Planned second corpus. Django migrations from 3-5 mid-sized open-source projects (Sentry, Mailman, Read the Docs, Saleor, Misago candidates). Target: 200-500 migrations with less defensive generator pre-emption than Prisma, enabling DM-15, DM-16, and DM-17 calibration attempts under the bar.
candidates.spec_reference: scripts/mvp-migration/MEASURED-CLAIMS.md section 'Calibration corpus #2 — spec for the next calibration source'
candidates.status_notes: Not yet sourced. Listed as candidate so the registry reflects the documented roadmap.
